blocks.text: Re: Portfolio Valuation for Perkins Group
Attention: Kyle Sanchez, VP Finance

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} has engaged Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP to appraise the commercial properties comprising the Perkins Group portfolio. The engagement covers each parcel identified in the schedule furnished by your office, and that schedule is the controlling statement of scope unless amended in writing. The intended use of our reports is credit and portfolio decision-making by the client and its lenders. The intended users are identified in each report's certification.

Each property will be inspected. We will develop the income, sales comparison, and cost approaches as the character of each parcel warrants, and we will reconcile the results in a self-contained appraisal report for each property. All work will conform to the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice and to the firm's internal review procedures. Conclusions will rest on documented comparables and on conditions observed at inspection, and each report will state what was relied upon.

Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, will direct the fieldwork and the reconciliation of each file. Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst, will assemble and verify the market data supporting each approach. David Ellison, Staff Appraiser, will conduct the inspections and maintain the working files. I will review every report and sign each certification. Requests for records and for access to the properties will be directed through your office.

Fieldwork will commence on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}, subject to access and to receipt of the rent rolls, operating statements, and title information for each parcel. The fee and the terms of payment are set out on the execution page of this letter, where they are executed by counsel. Consistent with the firm's practice, they are not restated in the body.
If the foregoing accurately states the engagement, please sign on the execution page and return one executed copy to this office. Questions may be directed to me.
Kickoff Memo: Portfolio Valuation for Perkins Group
To: Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst; David Ellison, Staff Appraiser. From: Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser.

The engagement letter is signed and the file is open. The client of record is {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}; the assignment is the valuation of the commercial parcels in the Perkins Group portfolio, one report per parcel, each prepared so a credit committee can rely on it without correction. Our start date under the letter is {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}. Nothing goes into a report that we cannot trace to a walked property or a documented record.

items: Records and access. Collect rent rolls, operating statements, leases, and site plans for every parcel. Route requests through Kyle Sanchez, VP Finance, at the client, and log what was requested and what was received.
Market research. Marcus Burnett pulls comparables for each property type, verifies each transaction with a party to it, and keeps verified data marked apart from data that is only reported.
Inspections. David Ellison schedules the site visits once access is confirmed and files field notes and photographs as each visit closes.
Reconciliation and review. I take each file front to back, reconcile the approaches, and stage the draft for partner review and signature.

blocks.text: Next steps: Marcus Burnett circulates the first comparable set for comment. David Ellison proposes an inspection sequence. I confirm the record request has gone out to the client. Raise problems in writing when you find them, not at the end.
Meeting Minutes: Portfolio Valuation for Perkins Group
Working session of the engagement team, held at the firm's office. Record prepared by David Ellison, Staff Appraiser. In attendance:

items: Kyle Sanchez, VP Finance, Perkins Group

blocks.text: Joseph Fisher took the files in order. Inspections are complete for every parcel released to us; my field notes and photograph logs are filed for each, and I have marked on my checklist where interior access was limited. I asked whether the limited-access parcels should be held open or written up with a stated limitation; Joseph Fisher directed that they be held open until access is resolved.

Marcus Burnett reported on the comparable set. Most transactions are verified with a direct party; the remainder are marked as reported and not yet confirmed, and one reported sale contradicts the county record. He will keep it out of the grids until the contradiction is resolved. Kyle Sanchez stated that {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} will re-issue the outstanding access and record requests to its property managers.

rows: Re-issue outstanding access and record requests to the property managers
Resolve the contradicted sale against the county record before it enters the grids
Schedule the remaining interior inspections once access is confirmed
Review reconciled drafts for the completed parcels

blocks.text: Status Report: Portfolio Valuation for Perkins Group
Prepared for {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} by Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP.
Status Summary

The engagement is on schedule. Inspections are complete for the full portfolio. The comparable set is closed and verified. The first group of reports has moved through reconciliation and internal review, and the balance is in process. Work performed to date is within the engagement budget executed with the engagement letter; no adjustment to the budget is requested.
Accomplishments This Period

items: Completed the remaining interior inspections. David Ellison closed out his inspection checklist for each parcel; field notes and photograph logs are filed.
Closed the comparable set. Marcus Burnett verified every transaction relied upon with a direct party; sales that could not be verified were excluded from the grids.
Reconciled the first group of files and moved them through internal review without reopening any fieldwork.

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

items: Reconcile the remaining files in the order their records closed.
Partner review and certification of each report.
Delivery of the completed reports for credit committee use.

blocks.text: Risks

One title question raised at inspection is with the client for confirmation. It does not affect the schedule at this time; if confirmation is delayed, the affected report will move to the end of the delivery order rather than hold the balance of the portfolio. No other open items affect schedule, scope, or the engagement budget.
